title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tens of thousands rallied at Boston Common for the third 'No Kings' protest, part of a nationwide series.
The event included speeches by Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren, and music from the Dropkick Murphys.
Over 160 such rallies were held in Massachusetts, with protestors advocating for democracy and against oppression.

title: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 17-song, 90-minute set at House of Blues Boston, featuring theatrical shock rock and heavy metal.
The band's performance included spraying fake blood and executing satirical acts on stage, delighting the nearly full audience.
Highlights included a Chappell Roan cover 'Pink Pony Club' and tributes to founding member Dave Brockie, aka Oderus Urungus.

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum, Neemias Queta, and Derrick White are listed as questionable for the Celtics' Sunday game.
Nikola Vucevic will remain out due to a right ring finger fracture for the upcoming game against the Hornets.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla described Brown as 'day-to-day' with left Achilles tendinitis ahead of Sunday's matchup.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands attended the 'No Kings' protest in Boston Common against the direction of the country and concerns about democracy.
Speakers included Governor Maura Healey and Senators Ed Markey and Elizabeth Warren, with performances by the Dropkick Murphys.
Protesters included veterans, church groups, and costumed participants, emphasizing rejection of concentrated power and monarchy.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands attended the 'No Kings' protest on Boston Common, one of over 160 events across Massachusetts.
Governor Maura Healey and Senators Edward Markey and Elizabeth Warren addressed the crowd at the Boston rally.
The protest, part of a nationwide movement, featured attendees marching through the Public Garden and Boston Common.

title: Photos: ‘No Kings’ sparks protests in Europe
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: 'No Kings Day' protests occur in Madrid, Paris, Rome, and Amsterdam, opposing Donald Trump and far-right policies.
'No Kings' movement originated in the U.S. as a major opposition to Trump's second term starting January 2025.
Massachusetts hosts over 160 protests out of 3,000 nationwide, second only to California in number of events.

title: Bravo Just Dropped the first full “Real Housewives of Rhode Island” Trailer. Here’s everything we learned.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 at 9 p.m. on Bravo, set in New England.
Alicia Carmody, running Pizza Mamma with her fiancé, and Liz McGraw, a dispensary owner, are main cast members.
The series explores Rhode Island's social scene, focusing on friendships, feuds, and loyalties within tight-knit communities.

title: Hampden County Sheriff plans dozens of job cuts
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sheriff Nick Cocchi announces 50 job cuts in Hampden County Sheriff's Department to save $4 million after budget reductions.
Budget cuts follow a $26.5 million decrease in the fiscal year 2026 budget due to statewide funding issues.
Job cuts will affect police presence and response times in Springfield, West Springfield, Holyoke, and Palmer.

title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council approved $2.65 million for new firefighter gear after finding cancer-linked PFAS in the current equipment.
Equipping over 270 firefighters with safer PFAS-free gear follows intense debate and pressures from firefighters and residents.
Gov. Maura Healey's legislation phases out PFAS in firefighting gear, affecting manufacturers and sellers by January 2026.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley, co-founder of The Eagles, is an executive producer for the 'Henry David Thoreau' documentary airing on PBS.
Henley established The Walden Woods Project in 1990 to prevent commercial development near Thoreau's historic site.
The documentary features voiceovers from George Clooney, Jeff Goldblum, and Meryl Streep, directed by the Ewers brothers.
